input.command: ls .ddx/executions/20260502T230833-36ef6add/ 2>/dev/null && ddx bead show ddx-5d49b14e 2>&1 | head -50
input.description: Check evidence dir and bead state

data.output:
ID:       ddx-5d49b14e
Title:    Evaluate and adopt a real backend for the bead tracker (axon primary, bd fallback)
Type:     epic
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2, area:beads, area:storage, kind:strategy, backend-migration
Owner:    ddx
Parent:   ddx-b18d9ce6
Desc:     The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).
Accept:   1. TD authored for the axon-backend integration (data model mapping, claim/lock semantics, archive policy, multi-machine concurrency story). 2. Axon-backend implementation lands behind a feature flag or selectable backend in cli/internal/bead/backend.go. 3. Migration tool moves an existing .ddx/beads.jsonl + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl into the axon backend without data loss. 4. Existing chaos_test.go suite passes against the axon backend. 5. bd fallback documented (single artifact under docs/) showing how to wire bd as the backend if axon adoption stalls. 6. Performance: queue ops (ready/blocked/show) at parity or better than JSONL on the current 1100-bead archive scale.
Created:  2026-05-02 13:06:50
Updated:  2026-05-02 23:08:33
Claimed:  2026-05-02T23:08:33Z
Machine:  eitri

Create a new bead

Usage:
  ddx bead create <title> [flags]

Flags:
      --acceptance string    Acceptance criteria
      --description string   Description
  -h, --help                 help for create
      --labels string        Comma-separated labels
      --parent string        Parent bead ID
      --priority int         Priority (0=highest, 4=lowest) (default 2)
      --set stringArray      Set custom field (key=value, repeatable)
      --type string          Bead type (task, bug, epic, chore)

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

data.text: This bead is an epic with 6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (design doc, backend interface refactor, new backend impl, migration tool, chaos-test integration, fallback doc, perf benchmark). It fails the size check decisively, and the prior attempt produced only an artifact. Decomposing per the instructions.

input.command:
ddx bead create "TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:design,backend-migration" \
  --description "Author a technical design doc for adopting axon as the primary bead-tracker backend. Cover: data-model mapping from current bead schema (id, status, claim, events, deps) to axon collections; claim/lock semantics (replacing flock + git tracker-commit lock); archive policy (events array growth, when/how to externalize per ADR-004); multi-machine concurrency story (Story 14 federation prerequisite); JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br round-trip; failure modes and migration strategy. Land the doc as .ddx/td/TD-axon-bead-backend.md (or under docs/helix/ if the project convention dictates — check existing TDs first). No code changes." \
  --acceptance "1. TD doc lands at the correct in-repo path covering all six topics above. 2. Doc references ADR-004 / SD-004 / FEAT-004 and the lock-contention v1 patch (ddx-da11a34a). 3. Doc proposes a concrete backend interface (Go) for cli/internal/bead/backend.go that both JSONL and axon can implement. 4. Doc identifies the chaos_test.go contract the new backend must satisfy."

ddx bead create "Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:refactor,backend-migration" \
  --description "Refactor cli/internal/bead to expose a Backend interface (per the TD from ddx-6bb51a52) so the existing JSONL+flock+git store becomes one implementation among many. No behavior change yet — JSONL backend remains default. Add a config knob (e.g. .ddx.yml beads.backend or DDX_BEAD_BACKEND env) to select a backend. Existing tests must keep passing unchanged." \
  --acceptance "1. cli/internal/bead/backend.go defines the Backend interface (CRUD, claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, JSONL export/import). 2. Current JSONL implementation refactored behind the interface; no caller changes outward behavior. 3. Backend selection plumbed via config + env (default jsonl). 4. go test ./cli/... passes. 5. chaos_test.go runs against the JSONL backend through the interface." \
  --set "depends_on=ddx-6bb51a52"

ddx bead create "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:feature,backend-migration" \
  --description "Implement the axon-backed Backend (per ddx-6bb51a52 TD and ddx-bbdd7564 interface). Cover claim semantics, atomic writes, events append, archive policy, and JSONL export/import for bd/br round-trip. Selectable via the backend config knob. Behind a feature flag until ddx-3ec0chaos signs off." \
  --acceptance "1. cli/internal/bead/axon_backend.go implements the Backend interface. 2. All ops (create/show/update/close, dep add/remove/tree, list/ready/blocked, claim, events append, archive split, import/export) covered by unit tests. 3. Selectable via beads.backend=axon. 4. go test ./cli/... passes with backend=axon for the new unit tests."

ddx bead create "Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:tool,backend-migration" \
  --description "Add a one-shot migration command (e.g. ddx bead migrate --to axon) that reads .ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl and writes them losslessly into the axon backend. Idempotent; safe to re-run. Does not delete the source files (operator removes after verification)." \
  --acceptance "1. ddx bead migrate --to axon command exists and is documented in --help. 2. After migration, every bead (including events and archive entries) round-trips: ddx bead export | diff against pre-migration export = empty. 3. Idempotent: running twice produces no duplicates. 4. Test fixture under cli/internal/bead/testdata covers a representative beads.jsonl + archive."

ddx bead create "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:test,backend-migration" \
  --description "Parameterize cli/internal/bead/chaos_test.go (or add a sibling) to run the existing chaos suite against the axon backend in addition to JSONL. Fix any bugs uncovered in the axon backend until the suite is green." \
  --acceptance "1. chaos_test.go (or chaos_axon_test.go) exercises the axon backend with the same scenarios (concurrent claims, crashes, dep-graph integrity). 2. go test -run Chaos ./cli/internal/bead/... passes for both backends. 3. Any bugs found are fixed in axon_backend.go, not papered over in tests."

ddx bead create "Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring" \
  --type task --priority 3 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:docs,backend-migration" \
  --description "Single docs artifact (e.g. docs/bead-backends.md or under docs/helix/) describing how to wire bd as the bead-tracker backend if axon adoption stalls. Cover: the Backend interface contract, where a bd-backed implementation would live, JSONL interchange, and known tradeoffs (external dependency)." \
  --acceptance "1. One doc file lands under docs/ describing the bd fallback path. 2. Doc references the Backend interface from ddx-bbdd7564 and the axon backend from ddx-95ec5ed5 for contrast. 3. Doc lists explicit wiring steps a future implementer would follow."

ddx bead create "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" \
  --type task --priority 2 --parent ddx-5d49b14e \
  --labels "phase:2,area:beads,area:storage,kind:perf,backend-migration" \
  --description "Add Go benchmarks for queue ops (ready, blocked, show) against both backends loaded with the current 1100-bead archive scale (or a synthesized fixture matching that shape). Capture baseline numbers for JSONL and axon, then verify axon is at parity or better. Commit baseline numbers as evidence under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/." \
  --acceptance "1. cli/internal/bead/bench_test.go (or similar) defines Benchmarks for ready/blocked/show against both backends. 2. Fixture loader builds a 1100-bead store. 3. Evidence file under .ddx/executions/<run-id>/perf-results.md records ns/op for both backends and confirms axon ≤ JSONL on each op. 4. If axon is slower, the bead is held open with reason in execute-bead notes — do not declare success."

ddx bead dep add ddx-bbdd7564 ddx-6bb51a52 && \
ddx bead dep add ddx-95ec5ed5 ddx-bbdd7564 && \
ddx bead dep add ddx-cb63cdfc ddx-95ec5ed5 && \
ddx bead dep add ddx-743bc194 ddx-95ec5ed5 && \
ddx bead dep add ddx-6602559c ddx-95ec5ed5 && \
ddx bead dep add ddx-8835a765 ddx-bbdd7564

ddx bead update ddx-5d49b14e --notes 'decomposed into ddx-6bb51a52 (TD), ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface), ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon impl), ddx-cb63cdfc (migration tool), ddx-743bc194 (chaos tests), ddx-8835a765 (bd fallback doc), ddx-6602559c (perf bench). Original epic was too big: 6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems (design, refactor, new impl, migration, tests, docs, perf). Children form a DAG: TD → interface → {axon impl, bd doc}; axon impl → {migration, chaos, perf}.'

input.content:
status: needs_investigation
reason: Bead ddx-5d49b14e is an epic that fails the size check decisively — six acceptance criteria span unrelated subsystems (technical design doc, backend-interface refactor, new axon backend implementation, migration tool, chaos-test integration, fallback documentation, performance benchmarking). The prior attempt produced only an execution result.json artifact and was BLOCKED in post-merge review for that reason. Per the execute-bead instructions, when a bead is too big the correct action is to decompose, not to attempt the work.

Decomposed into seven children (DAG):

  ddx-6bb51a52  TD: design axon-backend integration for bead tracker
  ddx-bbdd7564  Introduce selectable bead Backend interface in cli/internal/bead     (depends on ddx-6bb51a52)
  ddx-95ec5ed5  Implement axon Backend for bead tracker                              (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-cb63cdfc  Migration tool: .ddx/beads.jsonl + archive → axon backend            (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-743bc194  Run chaos_test.go against axon backend                              (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)
  ddx-8835a765  Document bd/doltdb fallback backend wiring                          (depends on ddx-bbdd7564)
  ddx-6602559c  Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive scale (depends on ddx-95ec5ed5)

Each child is independently sized for one execute-bead pass with concrete, verifiable ACs. The parent epic ddx-5d49b14e should remain open as the rollup; close it once all seven children land and post-merge review passes on each.

Follow-up note for triage: the lock-contention v1 patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains the stability scaffolding while this epic plays out — it is not blocked by this decomposition.

data.text:
Decomposed the epic into seven children with dependency edges, recorded the decomposition on the parent's notes, and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` (status: needs_investigation) listing each child ID and slice. No commit — a clean decomposition is the successful outcome here per the execute-bead size-check contract.

- `ddx-6bb51a52` TD design
- `ddx-bbdd7564` Backend interface refactor (→ TD)
- `ddx-95ec5ed5` axon backend impl (→ interface)
- `ddx-cb63cdfc` migration tool (→ axon impl)
- `ddx-743bc194` chaos tests (→ axon impl)
- `ddx-8835a765` bd fallback doc (→ interface)
- `ddx-6602559c` perf bench (→ axon impl)
